channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. — 36 replies — 144.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-06-12 01:33 UTC

OP

Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. Look at this poster who copies and pastes an entire thread just to shit it up and make fun of XRP holders: >>62338460

The funny thing? He forgot to remove the (You) when he copied and pasted the whole thing. But you can see his ID is different.

So my best guess is it's probably a single poster who constantly switches id to force this shitty meme. Probably a chainlink bagholder.

Early replies (first 3)

62339778
Imagine caring this much, holy shit. Adult bully victim no doubt

62339781
>>62339778
Lol of course I don't care so much but it goes to show how pathetic those link tards are trying to force that unfunny boom-xrp meme. The guy thinks if he switches id it looks like more people are in on it but that retard forgot to remove the (You) from his own post.

You are just butthurt you got called out hard and i'm gonna show this to everyone on /biz/ so they know who is really responsible for forcing that dumb meme.

62339829
>>62339778
idk bout that... xrp apparently lives rent free in linkbaggies minds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62340182
>>62339768
Its biepum the yeet node operator
Give him a break, chainlink shilling and xrp fudding is his personality

62340345
>>62339768
so fxcking bullish

62340350
Boooomers and peelers won

62340365
>>62339768
>Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.
GIGABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62340374
>>62339768
Why would anyone buy Chainlink or xrp I don't understand. Crypto is there to buy drugs online. Can you buy drugs online with Chainlink ? No. Can you buy drugs online with xrp? No. Case closed.

62340393
>>62339768
I don't hold link either tho
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62340516
everybody hates u dumb stinks

62340978
>>62339781
>words words words
It's funny how you're immediately identifiable by the spergy seethe that radiates from your posts.

Even funnier, >>62339844 posting the madmen meme.
>"We don't think about Linkies at all!!"
>Op literally creates a thread ranting about Chainlink because some other random thread that doesn't mention Chainlink could have been made by a Linkie

62340989
>>62340978
>TLDR linkjeet seethe

didnt read lol

62340992
BOOOOOOOOOOM

BULLISH AT XRP

62341232
Bumping this bullsih thread
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62342495
>>62339768
>My retarded shitposts made someone so mad he made an expose thread on me
FXCKING BULLISH KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62343674
>>62339768
>>62339781
>>62339835
>>62340989
>linkcucks and xrpaypigs having a retarded biz slapfight as they both go to 0

62344317
KABOOOOOOOOM>>62339768

62344795
>>62339781
This. I don't hold either but the link spam is so fucking stupid and annoying. They got decimated in a war length campaign of threads and saw zero wins and now it is over and over with this same shitty failed meme that ironically got funny when XRP posters just did it back to them to mock them. I have zero dog in this race but XRP is the clear winner in the messaging war.

62344908
>>62344795
>t. the same person a different id
manufacturing consensus on a dead board is pretty cringe desu senpai

62345267
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
link

62345354
Except XRP is actually being used

62347256
KABOOOOOOOOM

62347334
>>62344795
BOOOOOOOOOOM YOU HOLD XRP

62347434
>>62339781
>”I don’t care”
>paragraph about how much he cares

62347439
>>62339768
Both LINK and XRP are gay

62348331
I mean…it’s pretty hard not to cope and seethe with pictures like this

62348340
>>62348331
Must....not......boom...must...not.......
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
BULLISH AT THE TICKER XRP

62349668
>>62344908
You are incapable of conceiving of someone who just calls out your hypocrisy and annoying shilling. This is why you are how you are and you are like this because you do this. It is circular and self-feeding and swallowing. You are in a vortex, accusing me of doing exactly what you are in an even more retarded way than you are even accusing me of doing.
>>62347334
See the above reply. Same applies to you.

62349808
>>62349668
You are the verbose xrp shill though. You made this >>62349672 reply to the xrp general minutes after this one here in the same verbose style.

62349917
>>>62349808
Mentally ill VPN linkbaggie

62350205
>>62349808
It is indeed extremely obvious whenever he posts. Lately he is always talking about "muh XRP kept it's gains from the Trump pump!" yet XRP is down 70% from the ATH it made during said pump. Insanely delusional.

62354048
Hello linkers and ripplies. Actually, XRPL uses Chainlink.
XRP is powered by Chainlink.

62354051
Why is link and Cardano falling at the same time?

item.title: spaceX IPO — 30 replies — 120.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
spaceX IPO

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-12 01:33 UTC

OP

are we ready for the biggest scam in history of histories?

ELON MUSK, YOU FAGGGOT

when will your ponzi scheme finally collapse, you fucking hobo

Early replies (first 3)

62351469
>>62351461
like 8 different brokers and crypto apps have shilled this shit to me fucking lmao

are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?

62351500
It's actually going to be a repeat of the ICP ico isn't it?

62351509
>>62351469
>are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?
supposedly this is entering indexes in 15 days

total scam of scams. can't believe this is fucking happening. everyone (ameriburgers) about to get raped

i'm not american but I would've voted for the faggot. but this...
it would've either been: continuation of woke lunacy or scams

hard times
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AEB6ibtdPZc

>are they gonna
the cult is real. it will pump and potentially crater with ai bust in 6-12 months (or god knows when or if)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62351513
SpaceX IPO is literally the end of the stock market in both name and theory for the rest of all human history

62351521
>>62351461
Gaps to 300 on the first day, easily

62351530
>>62351461
21 hours till elon becomes worlds first trillionaire

62351534
>>62351461
I will post my gains here tomorrow.

62351545
>>62351461
>>62351500
>>62351513
Thanks bros, I just sold 10k off my portfolio to buy in :)

62351548
>>62351461
>Elmo Elongated Muskrat company bad
You do know that reddit has a stock board and they'll even give you reddit gold for saying such brave things?

62351550
>>62351509
>(or god knows when or if)
ai bust is going to happen

62351553
>>62351521
Slippage is gonna be insane on second one. I don’t see SPCX pricing below 200 after the first minute. Be sure to have a sizeable lump of cash if you are YOLOing.

62351559
>>62351548
>>Elmo Elongated Muskrat company bad
log off this board please

reddit and all liberal faggots were glazing this guy until he went Trumpster

MAGA BABY

62351572
>>62351530
whats even the point, the stupid nigger just works 24/7 and is CEO of 5 companies for fun or something who knows

like go spend your trillion dollars with those 10 kids and help them with their homework and take them on trips with the Private jet nigga wtf?? buy some 600M dollar mega yacht and have the kids go crazy while crossing the Atlantic on a mega mansion boat. Maybe find a nice woman your age to date and spend time with her too

62351576
>>62351548
about the only good thing this faggot did, was to put all the liberal dollars he received to semi decent use. compared to the lunacy on the other side
other than that, he's a mega shameful-autist scammer

62351581
>>62351572
He wants to build an army + his own country. He wants to liberate us from our oppressive governments. Poorfags like you wouldn’t understand

62351589
>>62351581
i guess bro

62351702
Surely it will go up for a brief moment once trading opens right?

62353489
I was around for the RIVIAN ipo in November 2021.
This is gonna be a similar shitshow

62353723
It might drop initially, but I think SpaceX is only going to get bigger in the long run.

62353776
Personally I think they’re gonna gigapump this one. It’s pretty suspicious that everything tanked a few days before the IPO. Not to mention the stupid index fund fast track.

62353811
>>62351572
>like go do retarded human animal bullshit that doesn't matter, like bro, go live a life that can be easily comprehended and respected by a stray dog, tickle your neurons with trivial pleasures which simpletons crave
kek
no

62353817
>>62351702

I thought that about Hawk Tuah coin, but here we are

62353874
>>62353489
"shitshow" is kind of an understatement, that would be trillions (plural) lost valuation after the initial pump

62353895
>>62353817
>Hawk Tuah coin
that had better fundamentals.

62353899
>>62351461
SpaceX is big scam ! Elon is nazi scammer ! I never buying SpaceX scam stocks , I am indian ,,

62353901
>>62353723
It's legitimately the most important company in the world lmfao. I've been waiting for this for a decade.

62354010
>>62351509
>continuation of woke lunacy
shut the fuck up retard, "woke lunacy" is better than the spite-filled shitshow that's happening now. white people feeling sad about being yesterday's news is not a valid political problem. kys pedophile

62354013
This shit is going to pump to ~$170.. I know Hyperliquid isn't the stock market but it's like poll where you only need a small sample to know the results of a bigger event.

62354047
>>62351461
BEHOLD

62354054
>>62351572
He is going to spend it all on making the US 95% Indian.

62354063
I remember tards on Yahoo finance saying Facebook stock would never be worth more than the IPO.

item.title: Going all in on SPCX IPO — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Going all in on SPCX IPO

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-12 01:26 UTC

OP

Wish me luck guys, how likely is a 2x intraday profit?

Early replies (first 3)

62352279
>>62352244
51% chance to the upside

62352474
Full retardation

62352493
>>62352244
do you have the shares or did you request them? its 5x over-subscribed there's no guarantee you get a single one.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62352534
>>62352244
You're gonna -80% 4 months later

62352614
>2x intraday
Won't happen

62352634
>>62352244
high but depends, retards will just market buy pumping the stock, once they see they can make some quick bucks they will market sell

62352754
>>62352244
99%, if it doesn’t work hold until the indexes rebalance, you should be in the green then

62352769
It's a long term investment. They are the first company to invest in mars. So once they start mining without any of the environmental regulations in the way, they will be worth quite a lot.

62352797
>>62352244

i think this is a retarded plan, ngl

62352835
>>62352244
Just know that you're investing in a fraud's company.

62353045
>>62352835
This applies to almost every stock available, you silly cunt

62353154
>>62352244
>2x
Nigger, you'll be lucky to 1x

62354030
I already made insane money today by owning Rocket Labs. It's going to be a good day tomorrow.

62353882
That is NOT 295k home

62353884
>>62353836

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

>>62353836
$300K for a below-average sized home in the suburbs of Kansas city is insane.

62353969
>>62353836
30k house in 1990

62354018
>>62353836
Sorry OP but this house is too gay for me

62354021
>>62353836
Wait are you saying $300k is the current affordable price for a starter home?

62354024
>>62354018
>TFW I will never be a secretly gay man in 1950s suburban Kansas
Why even live?!?

62354058

62354061
2 windows and a door, 300k
LMAO

item.title: the fucking jeets my managers hired have fucked me over. — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
the fucking jeets my managers hired have fucked me over.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-12 01:33 UTC

OP

the fucking jeets my managers hired have fucked me over. tamil fucks. they didnt put any data loggers as stipulated by sop into the fucking pallets they were packing and all the data loggers are fucking missing holy fuck
COO scheduled a meeting with me along with 3 different VP im so fucked holy fucking shit. 72 pallets of insulin is fucked. I only became head of APAC logistics 2 years ago fucks sake. I know they want to get rid of me, opportune. FUCK THIS.

Early replies (first 3)

62353920
>>62353896
you were too trusting. This is your mistake when you know they're trying to get rid of you. That's when you should have stepped up your game or started looking for a new job.

62353921
Im so sick of this. How is it my fault when they remove all my budget for manpower

62353940
>>62353896
they only know one type of log and its not the clean kind.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62353943
Stone Bridge Acquisition Corp? AIPAC?

62353962
>>62353896
whew lad
consider the enormous pricetag to replace the medicine the front-loaded cost of eventually replacing every white worker with a smelly jeet on 60% of your salary

62354001
>>62353896
humalog or lantus?

62354015
>>62353896
this is what jews do but in finance

62354045
White men are getting ousted from the labor force altogether. Now middle management (like you OP) are getting canned in mass #'s, the entry level blokes were first on the chopping block. Senior level "I think i'm PERMA COMFY IN MY POSITION I'M INVINCIBLE" types are next in line.

If you're C-suite, AI & H1B's (conveniently) leaves those alone, of course.

62354052
>>62354045
Why would they promote me when things are good only to can my ass when things arent what the fuck

item.title: >Iran completely shuts the strait >BTC goes up >Oil goes down Why is the market reacting positively to this news, when i… — 15 replies — 17.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Iran completely shuts the strait >BTC goes up >Oil goes down Why is the market reacting positively to this news, when i…

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-12 00:46 UTC

OP

>Iran completely shuts the strait
>BTC goes up
>Oil goes down
Why is the market reacting positively to this news, when it reacted negatively the first time this happened?

Early replies (first 3)

62349904
>>62349889
This is what broken markets do, the head of the Chicago CME warned about this. It's all fun and games for a while, until it breaks into a biblical catastrophe.
Which will happen end of summer.

62349914
The market is pricing in peace and a quick resolution to this small excursion.

62349976
>>62349904
We (yes, we) allowed a bunch of mentally ill hoarders, lolcows even, into power. Naturally, they made clown policies, which clown the markets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349979
>>62349976
Solution: Denying power. No more politburo commies, no more billionaires, no more politicians, behind seven layers of private scurity. Direct control and accountability for everyone.

62350027
>>62349979
well that will never happen

62350047
>>62349914

>Beginning of month 4 of quick resolution to this small excusion
>25 million dollar Apache helicopter destroyed by cheap drone with a lawnmower engine
>Hormuz is still closed

62350070
>>62350047
Americans deserve this for failing the emotional IQ test

62350079
>>62350070
*failing the emotional IQ test twice

62350618
>>62349889
Not really seeing the problem here tho

62350654
>>62349889
The market is like a woman, believing what they want to believe.

62351147
>>62349889
Hormuz is not an American problem. It's not even a goal of this war.

62352176
>>62350618
20% of the worlds oil comes out of the strait. The worlds manufacturing base in east asia relies on it. Some of east asia and most of europe is cut off from other major suppliers like Russia.

>>62351147
>muh WMDs
please

62353765
>>62352176
>20% of the worlds oil comes out of the strait.
It used to. Now it comes out of the Gulf of America. This really sounds like a (you) problem, Chang.

62353775
>>62349889
Exteme situation calls for extreme solution. Market demands blood.

62353887
>>62349889
>Iran completely shuts the strait
They haven't though, no matter how loudly they proclaim this. The US has been escorting roughly 30 tankers and container ships per day through the strait, and all Iran can do about it is pretend it isn't happening, and occasionally shoot down a helicopter.

item.title: ETF Recommendations — 30 replies — 17.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
ETF Recommendations

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-11 23:52 UTC

OP

Is it too late to buy some sector specific ETFs to try and ride the wave of this bull market
Obviously no one really knows what will happen, just wish I could have been investing a few years sooner
I already have a large standing in VOO but want to chase growth via sectors
My assumption was to not go into semiconductors or anything based on the computing of AI but things like energy that deal with the infrastructure of AI

Early replies (first 3)

62345926
I've rejected at least 3 east asian women. they're nice girls or whatever but they're not attractive. I exclusively am a latina/brown woman guy, i'm sorry.

62345956
>>62345926
Based. I like non inbred-looking women with a bit of meat on them too.

62345961
>>62345819
>this bull market
lol you are about to get heemed

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345983
>>62345961
why

62345996
I'm dating a half korean half south american, she's cute, low body count, people pleases the shit out of me (full body massage almost every time I see her) Amazing sex and we're like best friends. Problem is, I want white kids... or I could just be companions with her for the rest of my life and be childless. So I have been going through a crisis in figuring out what to do. Thanks for reading my blog, feel free to roast me or give an opinion

62346078
>>62345926
>>62345996
larp

62346171
>>62345996
my uncle married a chinese woman, they had 2 hapas togethe, the wife was pretty weird and extremely overprotective of the kids (e.g. she wouldn't let them play alone in their fully fenced back yard, even when they were 10+ years old).
They got divorced a few years ago and my aunt moved back to china with her daughter, and my cousin now hates his dad and has trooned out and posts pictures of him wearing dresses on instagram.

62346192
>>62345996
Just have the kids with her. My wife and I are pure white and your mutt daughter can pop out my son's 3/4 white kids on the side while he takes a break from his pure white wife if needed to help restore your bloodline.

62346478
>>62346192
lmao
>>62346171
this is pretty much my nightmare scenario
>>62346078
have sex incel asian girls are easy to colonize

62346501
>>62346478
what part of what i said even sounds like being an incel lol

62346508
>>62345996
Fuck, I have the same dichotomy, I'd love white kids, either blonde or brunette (I have dark hair, grreen eyes, part of my family has blonde hair and green/blue eyes). I want to date and perhaps marry an asian but something about my children potentially not having bright eyes pushes me away.

62346587
>>62346501
not believing random anons could have an asian gf, sounds like projection or defensiveness.
>>62346508
basically this

62346703
>>62346587
ok anyway op

62346750
>>62345819
ETFs die this year unless highly specialized. VOO and QQQ are going to get killed or oroboros churn from further concentrations. Invest in bottlenecks like tungsten.

62346790
>>62346750
but why exactly can you explain

62348362
If you aren't playing SOXS/SOXL you are losing, and with PDT gone you can play both ways.

62348734
>>62346508
>potentially
lol

62349415
>>62346508
asian maid
blonde wife

62349524
>>62345819
I don't think it's too late. I have around 20% of my portfolio in semiconductors and healthcare sectors with decend profits. Soon space innovation sector might open so I'll slurp that too. Besides that I have no interest in other sectors like defence, consumer goods, fintech etc....

62349557
>>62345996
There is nothing wrong with hapa daughters. All the most popular Kpop starlets are colonies.

62350599
>>62349557
>colonies
?

62350606
>>62350599
Colonies are the result of something being colonized

62350616
>>62349557
The only famous hapa kpop idol is Danielle and her career is fucked. Don't bring up Somi or Vernon cause they aren't famous

62350640
>>62346508
>potentially
You need to accept that if you have kids with an Asian woman they’ll be Asian. They will not look white. They will not “be” white. They’ll be a constant reminder of that. They will not look like you basically at all. Is that what you want? I am white and I have one kid so far and my kid looks like me.
>Blonde hair
>Blue eyes
I could not imagine having it any other way.

62350661
>>62345996
A child that is 1/4 Korean, 1/4 South American and 1/2 white will come out looking like a South American "white."

62350862
>>62345996
marry and have kids with her you stupid retard

62351184
>>62350640
>>62350862
These are the two conflicting arguments I have with myself every day

62351458
>>62351184
maybe should have thought considered that before you thirsted for asian pussy you stupid fuck

62353669
EZ 100x

62353745
>>62345819
load up on some AVDV my nigger

item.title: Anyone remember the guy who swore Silver was special in that there's going to be supply shock due to an extreme shortage… — 6 replies — 14.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone remember the guy who swore Silver was special in that there's going to be supply shock due to an extreme shortage…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-12 01:12 UTC

OP

Anyone remember the guy who swore Silver was special in that there's going to be supply shock due to an extreme shortage?
>There's no way Silver is coming back down
>This time will be different!
>Repeat after me: SOUND. MONEY.
Where are you buddy?

Early replies (first 3)

62348287
you retards gotta wait until it goes below $50 to brag
this is just a minor dip

62348313
>>62348276
>>62348287
KABOOOOOOOOM BULLISH SILVER

62349417
Step right up, step right up, welcome back to the Grand Tent of “Now THIS Time It’s Definitely a Perpetual Silver Shortage™,” where the previous argument has been gently escorted offstage for wearing the same oversized certainty shoes again.
So the refined claim waddles in wearing a slightly nicer hat: “Okay fine, no permanent shortage… BUT structural deficits, rising industrial demand, suppressed supply growth, therefore MOON.” And the crowd goes “oooh” because it sounds like economics, but it’s still doing cartwheels over a net that isn’t there.
Let’s begin the clown disassembly.
First act: “structural deficit.”
Translation: sometimes demand > supply. Congratulations, you have discovered… Tuesday in commodities. Every cyclical metal has moments where the ledger looks tight. Then price rises, recycling shows up, mines stretch output, demand quietly steps back like someone realizing popcorn is $18. The deficit doesn’t “accumulate into destiny,” it gets smacked by price signals like a clown hit with a pie labeled Reality Adjustment.
Second act: “industrial demand explosion.”
Yes, silver is useful. So is electricity. Neither means infinite uncontested growth. Solar panels already whisper sweet nothings like “please use less silver per unit,” because engineers are allergic to bankruptcy. Efficiency improvements are the market’s way of saying “nice narrative, but we’re not funding it forever.”
Third act: “supply is suppressed.”
Ah yes, the mining industry—famous for hating profit and refusing to expand when prices rise. Except… it does. Slowly, messily, imperfectly, but it does. And since silver often rides shotgun as a byproduct of copper and lead, it doesn’t even get to fully control its own destiny. It’s not a trapped genie—
Final act: “therefore moon.”
And
The only thing permanently in short supply here… is the shortage that refuses to behave like a cycle.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62352660
BTC goes to $120k, then pulls back to $60k
Silver goes to $120, then pulls back to $60

62352761
>>62352660
>BTC pulls back after GME announces buy-in
>Silver follows GME's movements almost to the day, 5 years later

62353987
>>62348276
>Where are you buddy?
Silverendsthefed.com

item.title: (srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb? — 8 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-12 00:38 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

Early replies (first 3)

62349969
you need to factor in the wc which is the biggest npc bread & circus event in modern history

62349972
>>62349960
i just matched up the timelines for 1973 and 2000 btw, that's where I'm getting the play by play for things. I reckon the trading AI's are probably averaging the two together given the macro environment

62349980
>>62349969
ah good point, ya so that might erase the june 17 - june 22 fud period and we go straight into june 22 - oct 10 period

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350015
>>62349960
Eh. I think they'll just turn shit up to 11 because Bank of America lost.

62352238
biz, so far my prediction is working, stop posting on 1pbtid pepe threads, and let me know if my predictions are shit or not

62353716
snailposting this shit since you retards only want to respond to 1pbtid's

62353764
>>62353716
It'll be a lost decade. It'll take 5-7 years for first new nuclear reactors to come online to unclog the the backlog of ai data centers that will be getting shit canned later this year or next after the crash.

Commodities will be the main play. World needs real resources to build infrastructure and robots. Shit doesn't mine itself out of the ground.

62353866
>>62353764
thanks, yea, seems to be that way. Right around 1973 market peak to 1975ish, gold picked up a 2x bid, so I imagine other commodities would follow suit

It's hard to imagine though, because I look around and I still to this day don't see labor inflation, yet everyone is speaking about how all this build out is happening. If it's happening, why doesn't it show up in labor? That's the first bottleneck that'll show up, no? It showed up in 08, are we saying all the mining and manufacturing just simply doesn't require that many humans? That seems a little bit farfetched to me

item.title: It's been over 6 months since we achieved AGI but there still have been no serious policies or discussions on providing… — 6 replies — 4.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
It's been over 6 months since we achieved AGI but there still have been no serious policies or discussions on providing…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-12 00:09 UTC

OP

It's been over 6 months since we achieved AGI but there still have been no serious policies or discussions on providing UBI or profit sharing mechanisms for the massive unemployed workforce we're about to see. If things don't change soon, we're headed towards a mass starvation event and destruction of over half of the world population. We need change now!

Early replies (first 3)

62353458
>>62353056
AGI isnt real

its literally Scifi and will never exist or if it will it wont be for another 250 years.

62353770
>>62353056
>have been no serious policies or discussions on providing UBI or profit sharing mechanisms
Why have discussions, serious or otherwise, about something that isn't gonna happen?

62353774
>>62353458
You're already dead, you just don't realize it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62353781
>>62353056
It's because the play isn't to roll out UBI or help the underclasses at all. The plan is to cull the herd.

62353789
>>62353781
this.

AGI+Robots = total goyim death

the plan is to hunt you down with drones like an animal

62353793
>>62353056
No one is giving you a free blank check because you're dumber than a GPU.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 191 replies — 13.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 191 • Updated: 2026-06-11 11:46 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

62340953
>>62340347
>But his timing is fully transparent.


classic boyscout bold faced lie

there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales other than them doing it 4 times per year. often it will be completely different months as years past.

didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol

62340971
>>62340953
>there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales
- always around 7% of total supply annually
- always on Fridays
- always within 3 to 4 month intervals

62342184
>>62340953
>didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol
Those are the transactions I posted that >>62340971 is trying to brush off as "part of the plan"

He's moving the goal post even when this discussion just bolsters my point about the link token price not accurately reflecting network usage. I initially wasn't thinking of the "routine token unlocks" so I'll give you my thanks.

62342243
>>62342184
You're claiming he unlocked right before the 10/10 crash to "capitalize on market movements".

The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval. Like he's done for years.

62342296
>>62342243
Oh no no no, he most certainly did unlock just before the crash. It was part of the panic and it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis.

62342402
>>62342296
>he most certainly did unlock just before the crash
Who said he didn't?

>it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis
It was luck BECAUSE he'd already been selling on a 3-4 month basis for years at that point.

62342424
Reminder you must only say positive things, otherwise you are a shill.

62342440
>>62342424
say something nice about chainlink

62342501
>>62313866
>>62313871
Glad to see some of us are still here... I didn't do the presale but t0 Binance buyer.
It's absolutely grim the state of this project.

62342517
>>62342243
>The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.

yeah they do it once a quarter
this particular one we're talking about just happened to be directly before one of the largest selloffs in industry history

>but duuuuuuuuude he always does it like that

no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months. so no it is not just like previous years.

62342559
>>62342517
>no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months
That's because he's unlocking every 3 to 4 months. Not in the same month every time.
He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.

62342602
>dumps straight on the most shady CEX are "unlocks"
peak doublespeak

62344923
>>62342602
All of Chainlink's tokens go through Binance, including node payments.
Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

62346060
>>62342559
>He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.


not sure why you keep repeating this as if this implies some transparency.

>duuuuuuuuuude just hang out on the blockchain every friday and you'll see it coming

its ok to concede they don't want to be transparent here. if they did they would just post a set schedule.

>>62344923
>Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

no one falls for this outside of the biggest retard boyscouts
where do the coins go?

62346092
>>62346060
Unlocking the same amount annually for years, within the same interval for years, and even around the same time of day for years implies transparency, yes.

>where do the coins go?
among others to the nodes

62346134
link blowz

62349168
Posting in the best LINK thread now. Some good convos. I hope we all make it.

62350386
>>62349168
its actually starting to reverbate with the muh influencers, they actuall read this thread, it's a good thing my goal is reached im happy

62351868
I haven't worked in exactly one year. I'm about to fuck my gf and go to bed now (Thailand time zone).

62351890
55/hr to sit on a construction site for 8 hrs a day. About 1 hrs worth of paperwork a day. I'm slowly losing my mind but trying to stick it out until my portfolio is big enough to NEET off of.

62351902
>>62351325
im getting paid like $50 an hour right now and siting in my underwear

62352109
reporting in from my remote cage

62352139
Hiding in my car I don't feel like working today. Definitely don't see the point in it anymore!

62352173
>>62351325

> wage

62352194
>>62351330

62352340
>>62351325
>>62351890
Just woke up. Drinking tea and playing Rainbow 6 Siege. Making $100 an hour. Made $800 sleeping 8 hours last night.

62352347
>>62351325
Just fuck my shit up senpai

62352360
>>62351325
Hi bros
>>62352340
How?

62352395
Prosecutor. Wasting time at work. Waiting for tomorrow to buy another $4000 in LINK when my paycheck lands. Feels good man.

62352400
I am employed but I’m not working today.

62352412
>>62352400
So you have a random Thursday off?
So you don’t even work a white collar 9-5? ngmi.

62352476
>>62351325
been waging since 8am EST. going to be here until 7pm. this is day 4 of 4 for the week thank god.

t. family doctor

62352824

62352850
>>62352412
I am off Thursday and Friday. work from home Saturday through Wednesday.

62352871
>>62352824

>three times
what are u doing

62352886
>>62352871

62353251
>>62351325

62353364
>>62352824

62353387

62353397
